comment_text.sn12.62:3.1: Here the abstract principle stands for the full set of links, which are not stated in this discourse.
comment_text.sn12.62:3.4: That is to say, certain kinds of contact are prone to create pleasant feelings, such as the contact experienced with a delicious taste, or in absorption.
comment_text.sn12.62:5.1: This sentence lists “contact” where, in the five aggregates, we would expect “form”. That’s because here it stands for the contact experienced through the senses, starting with the “eye”, which is included in “form”. A similar structure is found elsewhere, showing the linkage between these teaching frameworks (SN 35.121:15.1).

root_text.sn12.62:1.2: “Assutavā, bhikkhave, puthujjano imasmiṁ cātumahābhūtikasmiṁ kāyasmiṁ nibbindeyyapi virajjeyyapi vimucceyyapi.
root_text.sn12.62:1.4: Dissati, bhikkhave, imassa cātumahābhūtikassa kāyassa ācayopi apacayopi ādānampi nikkhepanampi.
root_text.sn12.62:1.5: Tasmā tatrāssutavā puthujjano nibbindeyyapi virajjeyyapi vimucceyyapi.
root_text.sn12.62:1.6: Yañca kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, vuccati cittaṁ itipi, mano itipi, viññāṇaṁ itipi, tatrāssutavā puthujjano nālaṁ nibbindituṁ nālaṁ virajjituṁ nālaṁ vimuccituṁ.
root_text.sn12.62:1.8: Dīgharattañhetaṁ, bhikkhave, assutavato puthujjanassa ajjhositaṁ mamāyitaṁ parāmaṭṭhaṁ:
root_text.sn12.62:1.10: Tasmā tatrāssutavā puthujjano nālaṁ nibbindituṁ nālaṁ virajjituṁ nālaṁ vimuccituṁ.
root_text.sn12.62:2.1: Varaṁ, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano imaṁ cātumahābhūtikaṁ kāyaṁ attato upagaccheyya, na tveva cittaṁ.
root_text.sn12.62:2.3: Dissatāyaṁ, bhikkhave, cātumahābhūtiko kāyo ekampi vassaṁ tiṭṭhamāno dvepi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno tīṇipi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno cattāripi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno pañcapi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno dasapi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno vīsatipi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno tiṁsampi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno cattārīsampi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno paññāsampi vassāni tiṭṭhamāno vassasatampi tiṭṭhamāno, bhiyyopi tiṭṭhamāno.
root_text.sn12.62:2.4: Yañca kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, vuccati cittaṁ itipi, mano itipi, viññāṇaṁ itipi, taṁ rattiyā ca divasassa ca aññadeva uppajjati aññaṁ nirujjhati.
root_text.sn12.62:3.1: Tatra, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako paṭiccasamuppādaṁyeva sādhukaṁ yoniso manasi karoti:
root_text.sn12.62:3.2: ‘iti imasmiṁ sati idaṁ hoti, imassuppādā idaṁ uppajjati;
root_text.sn12.62:3.3: imasmiṁ asati idaṁ na hoti, imassa nirodhā idaṁ nirujjhatī’ti.
root_text.sn12.62:3.4: Sukhavedaniyaṁ, bhikkhave, phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhavedanā.
root_text.sn12.62:3.5: Tasseva sukhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ sukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā sukhavedanā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn12.62:3.6: Dukkhavedaniyaṁ, bhikkhave, phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati dukkhavedanā.
root_text.sn12.62:3.7: Tasseva dukkhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ dukkhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā dukkhavedanā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn12.62:3.8: Adukkhamasukhavedaniyaṁ, bhikkhave, phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati adukkhamasukhavedanā.
root_text.sn12.62:3.9: Tasseva adukkhamasukhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ adukkhamasukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā adukkhamasukhavedanā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn12.62:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ kaṭṭhānaṁ saṅghaṭṭanasamodhānā usmā jāyati tejo abhinibbattati. Tesaṁyeva dvinnaṁ kaṭṭhānaṁ nānākatavinibbhogā yā tajjā usmā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati;
root_text.sn12.62:4.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, sukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhavedanā.
root_text.sn12.62:4.3: Tasseva sukhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ sukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā sukhavedanā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati …pe…
root_text.sn12.62:4.6: Tasseva adukkhamasukhavedaniyassa phassassa nirodhā yaṁ tajjaṁ vedayitaṁ adukkhamasukhavedaniyaṁ phassaṁ paṭicca uppannā adukkhamasukhavedanā sā nirujjhati sā vūpasammati.
root_text.sn12.62:5.1: Evaṁ passaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavā ariyasāvako phassepi nibbindati, vedanāyapi nibbindati, saññāyapi nibbindati, saṅkhāresupi nibbindati, viññāṇasmimpi nibbindati;
root_text.sn12.62:5.2: nibbindaṁ virajjati, virāgā vimuccati, vimuttasmiṁ vimuttamiti ñāṇaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn12.62:5.3: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānātī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.62:1.2: “Mendicants, an unlearned ordinary person might become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed from this body made up of the four principal states.
translation_text.sn12.62:1.4: This body made up of the four principal states is seen to accumulate and disperse, to be taken up and laid to rest.
translation_text.sn12.62:1.5: That’s why an unlearned ordinary person might become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed from it.
translation_text.sn12.62:1.6: But an unlearned ordinary person is unable to become disillusioned, dispassionate, or freed from that which is called ‘mind’ and also ‘sentience’ and also ‘consciousness’.
translation_text.sn12.62:1.8: Because for a long time they’ve been attached to it, thought of it as their own, and mistaken it:
translation_text.sn12.62:1.9: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’
translation_text.sn12.62:1.10: That’s why an unlearned ordinary person is unable to become disillusioned, dispassionate, or freed from it.
translation_text.sn12.62:2.1: But an unlearned ordinary person would be better off taking this body made up of the four principal states to be their self, rather than the mind.
translation_text.sn12.62:2.3: This body made up of the four principal states is seen to last for a year, or for two, three, four, five, ten, twenty, thirty, forty, fifty, or a hundred years, or even longer.
translation_text.sn12.62:2.4: But that which is called ‘mind’ and also ‘sentience’ and also ‘consciousness’ arises as one thing and ceases as another all day and all night.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.1: In this case, a learned noble disciple carefully and rationally applies the mind to dependent origination itself:
translation_text.sn12.62:3.2: ‘When this exists, this comes to be; due to the arising of this, this arises.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.3: When this doesn’t exist, this doesn’t come to be; due to the cessation of this, this ceases.’
translation_text.sn12.62:3.4: Pleasant feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as pleasant.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.5: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as pleasant, the corresponding pleasant feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.6: Painful feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as painful.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.7: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as painful, the corresponding painful feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.8: Neutral feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as neutral.
translation_text.sn12.62:3.9: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as neutral, the corresponding neutral feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn12.62:4.1: When you rub two sticks together, heat is generated and fire is produced. But when you part the sticks and lay them aside, any corresponding heat ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn12.62:4.2: In the same way, pleasant feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as pleasant.
translation_text.sn12.62:4.3: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as pleasant, the corresponding pleasant feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn12.62:4.5: Neutral feeling arises dependent on a contact to be experienced as neutral.
translation_text.sn12.62:4.6: With the cessation of that contact to be experienced as neutral, the corresponding neutral feeling ceases and stops.
translation_text.sn12.62:5.1: Seeing this, a learned noble disciple grows disillusioned with contact, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn12.62:5.2: Being disillusioned, desire fades away. When desire fades away they’re freed. When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.sn12.62:5.3: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
